g.text: Optic hierarchy — every optic is a restriction of Traversal

div.p: An optic focuses on part of a data structure. The hierarchy goes from most general (Traversal) to most specific (Lens/Iso):

ul.li: Iso: isomorphic pair — get and review are inverses
Lens: exactly one product-type field — always get, always set
Prism: one sum-type constructor — may fail to get; can always construct
Traversal: zero or more targets — fold or map over all of them

div.p: Optics compose with (.) — the same function composition from Ch. 2. A Lens composed with a Traversal produces a Traversal.

g.title: Isomorphism between s and a — can convert both ways with no information loss.
Focus on exactly one field in a product type. Always succeeds.
Focus on zero or more targets. Compose Lens + Prism to get a Traversal.
Focus on one constructor of a sum type. Getting may return Nothing.
Read-only traversal — accumulate values but cannot update.
Single-target read-only focus.
Update focus — cannot read back.
Read the focused field from s.
Replace the focused field
Apply f to the focused field
